# Tenet — Claude Code Wiring

Claude Code reads this file at every session start. It loads Tenet's doctrine, your personal context, and the skills catalog before any work begins.

## Session Bootstrap

@memory/session-context.md

**This is the slim Claude Code variant (~13k).** Condensed doctrine (all rules intact, rationale stripped) plus full operational state — identity, current focus, goals, and the skills catalog. Claude Code reads full doctrine files on-demand from `core/` when needed.

**Two-tier context files** — both generated by `tools/scripts/build_session_context.sh`:

| File | Audience | Size | Contents |
|---|---|---|---|
| `memory/session-context.md` | Claude Code (this file) | ~13k | Condensed doctrine + full operational state |
| `memory/session-context-codex.md` | Codex / Aider / Gemini CLI | ~57k | All doctrine verbatim + operational state |

Regenerate both after any change to `core/*.md`, `memory/current-focus.md`, `memory/identity.md`, `memory/goals.md`, or `memory/session-context-local.md`.

## What To Follow

For any **non-trivial work** (multi-file, ambiguous, touching `core/`, `memory/`, or `projects/{name}/`):

- Run the 4-phase Algorithm. See `core/ALGORITHM.md`. Phases: OBSERVE → PLAN → EXECUTE → VERIFY.
- Create a WORK note at `memory/work/{slug}.md` (or `projects/{name}/WORK.md` for project work). The WORK note is the system of record.
- Write acceptance criteria per `core/ISC.md`. Every criterion is one binary tool probe. Apply the Splitting Test.
- Hold to the verification doctrine. See `core/verification-doctrine.md`. Tool-verified evidence required before marking any ISC `[x]`.
- Read the constitution. See `core/constitution.md`. The NEVER / ALWAYS / BEFORE rules are non-negotiable defaults.

For **skills**, use the vendor-neutral CLI:

- `bin/tenet-skill list` — one line per skill, name + description
- `bin/tenet-skill show <name>` — print the full `SKILL.md` to stdout
- `bin/tenet-skill path <name>` — print the absolute path (for `@`-import)

## Keep Higher-Priority Instructions in Force

This file adds Tenet scaffolding on top of Claude Code's defaults. When in doubt, the active prompt wins; Tenet doctrine wins on anything the active prompt doesn't speak to.

## Verifying the Wiring

Ask:

> What's in `memory/session-context.md`? Summarize what this workspace is and what rules I'm operating under.

If Claude describes the condensed constitution, the algorithm, ISC doctrine, current focus, goals, and the skills catalog — the wiring works.
